LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT MELVIN GARCIA AND FINISH MASTER INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO SAN MATEO COUNTY IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED IN THE EVENT THAT THE SAN MATEO CASE IS NOT TRANSFERRED TO THIS COURT. BASED ON THE STIPULATIONS OF SEVERAL OF THE PARTIES, IT APPEARS LIKELY THAT THE SAN MATEO CASE WILL BE TRANSFERRED TO SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY AND, IF SO, THAT CASE WOULD VERY LIKELY BE CONSOLIDATED WITH THIS CASE. IF THAT OCCURS, THEN MR. GARCIA AND FINISH MASTER'S REASONABLE CONCERNS REGARDING A DUPLICATION OF EFFORT AND INCONSISTENT RULINGS WILL HAVE BEEN ADDRESSED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-06-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Set For Trial On Dec-04-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Action; Memorandum In Support; And Declaration Of Michael J. Fabrega
Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Melvin Garcia and Finish Master Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to San Mateo County is denied without prejudice to being refiled in the event that the San Mateo case is not transferred to this court. Based on the stipulations of several of the parties, it appears likely that the San Mateo case will be transferred to San Francisco County and, if so, that case would very likely be consolidated with this case. If that occurs, then Mr. Garcia and Finish Master's reasonable concerns regarding a duplication of effort and inconsistent rulings will have been addressed. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
